content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

April 22, 1976

MR. John C. Petry
715 Patolita Drive
Corona del Mar, CA 92625

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 76-052

Dear Mr. Petry:

Thank you for your letter of April 7, 1976, requesting
an opinion with regard to the Los Angeles County Almanac.

No formal opinion will be issued because the question
you raise has been answered by an opinion of the Commission.
The opinion requested by William A. McCormick, 2 FPPC Ops.
42 (No. 75-140, March 16, 1976), concludes that the purchase
price of a Los Angeles County Almanac is not a contribution
to the Almanac Committee because the purchaser receives full
and adequate consideration for the purchase price. A copy
of that opinion is enclosed.

If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion
request, you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18321.
If you have any questions, please contact Natalie West, an
attorney with the legal Division of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein,
Chairman

DHL;pvp

Enclosure

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-6461 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5501 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

April 7, 1976


Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

     I would appreciate an opinion by the Commission on the
status of purchases made of the Los Angeles County Almanac published
by the Republican Central Committee of Los Angeles County.  The
Almanacs are subsequently contributed to various charitable organi-
zations.  It is my understanding that the subsequent contributions
qualify for a charitable deduction; however, there appears to be
some question as to whether or not the original purchase is a political
contribution.  If the price paid reflects the fair market value of
the Almanac, I would presume there is no political contribution.

     In any event, I would appreciate a written opinion from
the FPPC regarding this matter.  Thank you for your cooperation.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        John C. Petry

715 Patolita Drive
Corona del Mar, California 92625
